title: City Walk Outcomes - February 2026
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: March 23, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
DEPT: Community Engagement & Services
SUBJECT: City Walk Outcomes – February 2026
The City Walks Program is designed to enhance quality of life for residents while expediting service delivery to vulnerable populations.
Coordinated by Clean and Safe, City Walks are data-driven and strategically aligned with broader organizational initiatives. Using an interdepartmental approach, City Walks promote proactive problem-solving, timely interventions to address emerging Reno Direct service request trends, partners with community members to address concerns, and coordinates with the Reno Police Department's DEEDS (Directed Engagement and Enforcement Deployment Strategies) zones as a mechanism to connect with residents, businesses, services, and enforcement efforts.
City Walks take place four days per week, Monday to Thursday, including two days dedicated to City Walks within the Business Improvement District (BID) in partnership with the Downtown Reno Partnership.
City Walks Departments
• Community Engagement and Services – Clean and Safe
• Licensing and Code Enforcement
• Parks and Recreation – Park and River Rangers, based on location
• Reno Police – Community Action & Outreach (CAO), Mobile Engagement Team (MET), and Parking Enforcement
The following is an overview of City Walk operations and outputs for February 2026:
February 2026 Highlights

• 12 city walks
• 74 contacts with community members
• 8 yards of trash collected
• 7 occupied vehicles posted
• 4 citations
• 3 Code Enforcement cases created
• 3 city walks in DEEDS zone
Monday, February 2, DEEDS zone
• Locations: Oddie Blvd, Silverada Blvd, and Paradise Drive
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
o Parking Enforcement
• Outcomes:
o 2 individuals contacted and declined services
o 3 businesses contacted and informed about the Loitering Program
o 2 yards of trash were collected
Tuesday, February 3, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along the Truckee River behind Greater Nevada Field, Wells underpass, and Brodhead Park
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 4 individuals contacted and declined services
o 1 citation issued for sitting, lying, sleeping, or camping on a public place
Wednesday, February 4
• Locations: 8th Street Park, Record Street, 7th Street, Valley Road, and Beech Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 3 individuals contacted and declined services
o 6 nuisance vehicles issued 24-hour notice postings
o 2 vehicles cited for parking in the red zone
o 4 yards of trash collected
Thursday, February 5, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Evans Avenue, 4th Street, and Record Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 10 individuals contacted and declined services
Monday, February 9, DEEDS zone
• Locations: Oddie Blvd, Silverada Blvd, and Paradise Drive
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 11 individuals contacted and declined services
o 3 Code Enforcement cases created due to property maintenance or nuisance concerns
▪ Camping and debris on private property
Tuesday, February 10, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along the Truckee River behind Greater Nevada Field, Wells underpass, and Brodhead Park
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
o Park and River Rangers, Parks and Recreation
• Outcomes:
o 11 individuals contacted and declined services
Wednesday, February 11
• Locations: 9th Street, Quincy Street, 8th Street, Sutro Street, and 7th Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 4 individuals contacted and declined services
o 1 nuisance vehicle issued a 24-hour notice posting
Thursday, February 12, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Evans Avenue, 4th Street, and Record Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 10 individuals contacted and declined services
Monday, February 23, DEEDS zone
• Locations: Fantastic Drive, Silverada Blvd, and Oddie Blvd
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 1 individual contacted and declined services
Tuesday, February 24, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Along the Truckee River behind Greater Nevada Field, Wells underpass, and Brodhead Park
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
o Park and River Rangers, Parks and Recreation
• Outcomes:
o 6 individuals contacted
▪ 1 individual transported to Cares Campus
▪ 5 individuals declined services
o 2 citations issued
▪ 1 sitting, lying, sleeping, or camping on a public place
▪ 1 unlawful use or possession of drug paraphernalia
Wednesday, February 25
• Locations: Along Ferrari Street from 4th Street to 5th Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Code Enforcement
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 3 individuals contacted
▪ 1 individual transported to Mill Street Care Center for wraparound services
▪ 2 individuals declined services
o 1 citation issued for unauthorized removal of a shopping cart
o 2 yards of trash were collected
Thursday, February 26, with the Downtown Reno Partnership
• Locations: Vine Street, Third Street, Ralston Street, and 6th Street
• Departments in Attendance:
o Clean and Safe, Community Engagement and Services
o Reno Police Department
• Outcomes:
o 6 individuals contacted and declined services

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
fields.tldr: This memo reports on City Walk operations conducted during February 2026, covering 12 interdepartmental walks across Reno neighborhoods and the downtown Business Improvement District. Teams from Community Engagement & Services, Code Enforcement, Parks and Recreation, and Reno Police contacted 74 community members, collected 8 yards of trash, posted 7 occupied vehicles, issued 4 citations, and opened 3 Code Enforcement cases. City Walks are coordinated by Clean and Safe and strategically aligned with Reno Police DEEDS zones and the Downtown Reno Partnership to connect vulnerable residents with services and address quality-of-life concerns.
fields.key_stats: 12 city walks conducted; 74 community member contacts; 8 yards of trash collected; 7 occupied vehicles posted with 24-hour notices; 4 citations issued; 3 Code Enforcement cases created; 3 walks conducted in DEEDS zones; 1 individual transported to Cares Campus; 1 individual transported to Mill Street Care Center; 3 businesses informed about the Loitering Program

Parks and Recreation – Park and River Rangers
Reno Police Department – Community Action & Outreach (CAO)
Reno Police Department – Mobile Engagement Team (MET)

Business Improvement District (BID) partnership with Downtown Reno Partnership
people: Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
